devanagari: जिघांसोः कंसस्य हस्तात् उन्मुक्ताया देव्या भगवद् । अवतार सूचनं कंसानुतापःतदीय दुर्मंत्रिणां दुर्मंत्रणं च । श्रीशुक उवाच । बहिरन्तःपुरद्...
english_devnagari: jighāṃsoḥ kaṃsasya hastāt unmuktāyā devyā bhagavad avatāra sūcanaṃ kaṃsānutāpaḥtadīya durmaṃtriṇāṃ durmaṃtraṇaṃ ca śrīśuka uvāca bahirantaḥpuradvāraḥ sarvāḥ pūrvavadāvṛtāḥ tato bāladhvaniṃ śrutvā gṛhapālāḥ samutthitāḥ

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
(After the return of Vasudeva) all the gates of the outer and inner apartments became closed as before. Hearing the cry of the baby, the palace guards became wide-awake.

devanagari: ते तु तूर्णं उपव्रज्य देवक्या गर्भजन्म तत् । आचख्युः भोजराजाय यदुद्विग्नः प्रतीक्षते ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: te tu tūrṇaṃ upavrajya devakyā garbhajanma tat ācakhyuḥ bhojarājāya yadudvignaḥ pratīkṣate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ tvar class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ tvar class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: They, on their part, (promptly and) hastily approached Kaṃsa, the King of Bhojas, and reported (to him) Devakī’s delivery of that (eighth) child which he was expecting anxiously.

devanagari: स तल्पात् तूर्णमुत्थाय कालोऽयमिति विह्वलः । सूतीगृहं अगात् तूर्णं प्रस्खलन् मुक्तमूर्धजः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa talpāt tūrṇamutthāya kālo sūtīgṛhaṃ agāt tūrṇaṃ praskhalan muktamūrdhajaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ tvar class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ tvar class 1 verb]
[imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]
[nominative single from √ tvar class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ tvar class 1 verb]

translation: Springing up hurriedly from the bed and bewildered at the thought that that (child) was his (veritable god of) death, he, with tottering steps and dishevelled hair, rushed hastily to (Devakī’s) lying-in-room.

devanagari: तं आह भ्रातरं देवी कृपणा करुणं सती । स्नुषेयं तव कल्याण स्त्रियं मा हन्तुमर्हसि ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ āha bhrātaraṃ devī kṛpaṇā karuṇaṃ satī snuṣeyaṃ tava kalyāṇa striyaṃ mā hantumarhasi
translation: To her brother (Kaṃsa), the pious, divine, (yet) miserable lady piteously addressed. “This is your daughter- in-law, Oh blessed brother. She may be the eighth child (but) it is not proper that you should kill a female (child).

devanagari: बहवो हिंसिता भ्रातः शिशवः पावकोपमाः । त्वया दैवनिसृष्टेन पुत्रिकैका प्रदीयताम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: bahavo hiṃsitā bhrātaḥ śiśavaḥ pāvakopamāḥ tvayā daivanisṛṣṭena putrikaikā pradīyatām

translation: Dear Brother! Impelled as you were by Destiny, a number of my children brilliant like fire, were killed by you. Be pleased to give (spare) this one daughter to me.

devanagari: नन्वहं ते ह्यवरजा दीना हतसुता प्रभो । दातुमर्हसि मन्दाया अङ्गेमां चरमां प्रजाम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: nanvahaṃ te hyavarajā dīnā hatasutā prabho dātumarhasi mandāyā aṅgemāṃ caramāṃ prajām

[vocative single from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: Oh mighty king! Am I not your miserable younger sister who am bereaved of all my sons? Unfortunate as I am, it behoves you, Oh dear brother, to spare this last child for me”.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । उपगुह्य आत्मजां एवं रुदत्या दीन अदीनवत् । याचितस्तां विनिर्भर्त्स्य हस्ताद् आचिच्छिदे खलः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca upaguhya ātmajāṃ evaṃ rudatyā dīna adīnavat yācitastāṃ vinirbhartsya hastād ācicchide khalaḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
(Although) he was thus implored by Devakī while hugging the child to her bosom and weeping piteously like a most helpless woman, the wicked fellow sternly rebuked her and snatched away the baby from her.

devanagari: तां गृहीत्वा चरणयोः जातमात्रां स्वसुः सुताम् । अपोथयत् शिलापृष्ठे स्वार्थोन्मूलितसौहृदः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: tāṃ gṛhītvā caraṇayoḥ jātamātrāṃ svasuḥ sutām apothayat śilāpṛṣṭhe svārthonmūlitasauhṛdaḥ

translation: His affection (for the sister and niece) being uprooted (superseded) by his (vile) self-interest, he seized the new-born daughter of his sister by her feet and dashed her against the surface of a slab.

devanagari: सा तद्हस्तात् समुत्पत्य सद्यो देव्यंबरं गता । अदृश्यतानुजा विष्णोः सायुधाष्टमहाभुजा ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: sā tadhastāt samutpatya sadyo devyaṃbaraṃ gatā adṛśyatānujā viṣṇoḥ sāyudhāṣṭamahābhujā

translation: Flying off from (the grip of) of hands, she rose up to the sky and (stationing herself in the sky), the younger sister of Kṛṣṇa manifested herself as a goddess with eight mighty arms wielding weapons.

devanagari: दिव्यस्रग् अंबरालेप रत्‍नाभरणभूषिता । धनुःशूलेषुचर्मासि शंखचक्रगदाधरा ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: divyasrag aṃbarālepa rat‍nābharaṇabhūṣitā dhanuḥśūleṣucarmāsi śaṃkhacakragadādharā
translation: She was adorned with heavenly garlands, garments, pigments, jewelled ornaments and wielded (in her arms) a bow, a spear, an arrow, a shield, a sword, a conch, a discus and a mace.

devanagari: सिद्धचारणगन्धर्वैः अप्सरःकिन्नरोरगैः । उपाहृतोरुबलिभिः स्तूयमानेदमब्रवीत् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: siddhacāraṇagandharvaiḥ apsaraḥkinnaroragaiḥ upāhṛtorubalibhiḥ stūyamānedamabravīt

translation: She was being praised by Siddhas, Cāraṇas, Gandharvas, Apsaras (divine damsels), Kinnaras and serpents who offered her rich, sumptuous presents. She spoke:

devanagari: किं मया हतया मन्द जातः खलु तवान्तकृत् । यत्र क्वं वा पूर्वशत्रुः मा हिंसीः कृपणान्वृथा ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ mayā hatayā manda jātaḥ khalu tavāntakṛt yatra kvaṃ vā pūrvaśatruḥ mā hiṃsīḥ kṛpaṇānvṛthā

translation: “You dunce! What have you gained by killing me? Your destroyer, your adversary in the former birth, is born somewhere (in an unknown place). Do not unnecessarily kill helpless, innocent children.”

devanagari: इति प्रभाष्य तं देवी माया भगवती भुवि । बहुनामनिकेतेषु बहुनामा बभूव ह ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: iti prabhāṣya taṃ devī māyā bhagavatī bhuvi bahunāmaniketeṣu bahunāmā babhūva ha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Having warned him thus, the goddess (disappeared). The venerable goddess Māyā is known under many names and is worshipped in many shrines, bearing different names.

devanagari: तया अभिहितं आकर्ण्य कंसः परमविस्मितः । देवकीं वसुदेवं च विमुच्य प्रश्रितोऽब्रवीत् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: tayā abhihitaṃ ākarṇya kaṃsaḥ paramavismitaḥ devakīṃ vasudevaṃ ca vimucya praśrito
translation: Kaṃsa was extremely flabbergasted to hear her pronouncement. He set Vasudeva and Devakī at liberty and apologised in humility:

devanagari: अहो भगिन्यहो भाम मया वां बत पाप्मना । पुरुषाद इवापत्यं बहवो हिंसिताः सुताः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: aho bhaginyaho bhāma mayā vāṃ bata pāpmanā puruṣāda ivāpatyaṃ bahavo hiṃsitāḥ sutāḥ

translation: “Alas! My sister! Oh my brother-in-law! Like a cannibal preying on his own child, many a son of yours has been killed by me, a wicked sinner.

devanagari: स त्वहं त्यक्तकारुण्यः त्यक्तज्ञातिसुहृत् खलः । कान् लोकान् वै गमिष्यामि ब्रह्महेव मृतः श्वसन् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tvahaṃ tyaktakāruṇyaḥ tyaktajñātisuhṛt khalaḥ kān lokān vai gamiṣyāmi brahmaheva mṛtaḥ śvasan

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative single from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ mṛ class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ śvas class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ śvas class 2 verb]

translation: For being so, (a sinner) bereft of mercy, for disowning my friends and relatives, for being virtually dead though breathing (merely alive), I do not know to what infernal region I shall be taken as in the case of a slayer of Brāhmaṇas.

devanagari: दैवं अपि अनृतं वक्ति न मर्त्या एव केवलम् । यद् विश्रंभाद् अहं पापः स्वसुर्निहतवान् शिशून् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: daivaṃ api anṛtaṃ vakti na martyā eva kevalam yad viśraṃbhād ahaṃ pāpaḥ svasurnihatavān śiśūn

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ vā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ vā class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ vā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vā class 1 verb]

translation: Not only mortals but even the celestials also speak untruth. Putting faith in them, I, a wicked sinner, killed my sister’s children.

devanagari: मा शोचतं महाभागौ आत्मजान् स्वकृतंभुजः । जन्तवो न सदैकत्र दैवाधीनास्तदाऽसते ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: mā śocataṃ mahābhāgau ātmajān svakṛtaṃbhujaḥ jantavo na sadaikatra daivādhīnāstadā
translation: Oh high-souled ones! Do not lament for your sons who reaped the fruit of their previous deeds. Creatures are at the mercy of the Providence. They do not live or are not together (at the same place) for ever.

devanagari: भुवि भौमानि भूतानि यथा यान्ति अपयान्ति च । नायमात्मा तथैतेषु विपर्येति यथैव भूः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: bhuvi bhaumāni bhūtāni yathā yānti apayānti ca nāyamātmā tathaiteṣu viparyeti yathaiva bhūḥ
translation: Just as in this world earthen articles are created out of and (after destruction) become one with the earth, but the earth itself persists (unmodified), similarly in the process of creation and destruction of physical bodies the Soul undergoes no modifications.

devanagari: यथानेवंविदो भेदो यत आत्मविपर्ययः । देहयोगवियोगौ च संसृतिर्न निवर्तते ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: yathānevaṃvido bhedo yata ātmaviparyayaḥ dehayogaviyogau ca saṃsṛtirna nivartate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ yam class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ yam class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ yam class 1 verb], [locative single from √ yam class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ yam class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ yam class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ yam class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ yam class 1 verb]

translation: Misapprehension about the nature of the Soul arises in him who does not understand the Reality as it is. That leads to the notion of difference (contrary to the real nature of the Soul). Thus there is no end to saṃsāra and to the union and separation from the body (i.e. the cycle of births and deaths).

devanagari: तस्माद्‍भद्रे स्वतनयान् मया व्यापादितानपि । मानुशोच यतः सर्वः स्वकृतं विन्दतेऽवशः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmād‍bhadre svatanayān mayā vyāpāditānapi mānuśoca yataḥ sarvaḥ svakṛtaṃ vindate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Inasmuch as every creature has inevitably and helplessly to reap the fruit of his actions, Oh blessed sister, do not grieve over (the death of) your sons, even though they were killed by me.

devanagari: यावद्धतोऽस्मि हन्तास्मीति आत्मानं मन्यतेऽस्वदृक् । तावत् तद् अभिमान्यज्ञो बाध्यबाधकतामियात् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: yāvaddhato tāvat tad abhimānyajño bādhyabādhakatāmiyāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb]
[present middle third single], [present passive third single]

translation: So long as a person does not cognize the real nature of his Self and identifies his body with the Soul, and (wrongly) regards himself as being killed or being the killer, that ignorant fellow is subject to the role of being the subject or object of suffering.

devanagari: क्षमध्वं मम दौरात्म्यं साधवो दीनवत्सलाः । इत्युक्त्वाश्रुमुखः पादौ श्यालः स्वस्रोः अथाग्रहीत् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣamadhvaṃ mama daurātmyaṃ sādhavo dīnavatsalāḥ ityuktvāśrumukhaḥ pādau śyālaḥ svasroḥ athāgrahīt
translation: Saintly persons are compassionate to the afflicted and the poor. Please do forgive me for my wickedness.” With these words and with his face overflowing with tears, he clasped the feet of his sister and her husband.

devanagari: मोचयामास निगडात् विश्रब्धः कन्यकागिरा । देवकीं वसुदेवं च दर्शयन् आत्मसौहृदम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: mocayāmāsa nigaḍāt viśrabdhaḥ kanyakāgirā devakīṃ vasudevaṃ ca darśayan ātmasauhṛdam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ dṛś ], [vocative single from √ dṛś ]

translation: Being reassured by the words of the daughter of Devakī, Kaṃsa, as a testimony of his personal affection to them, released Vasudeva and Devakī from chains (prison).

devanagari: भ्रातुः समनुतप्तस्य क्षान्त्वा रोषं च देवकी । व्यसृजत् वसुदेवश्च प्रहस्य तमुवाच ह ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: bhrātuḥ samanutaptasya kṣāntvā roṣaṃ ca devakī vyasṛjat vasudevaśca prahasya tamuvāca ha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Due to the (sincere) regrets and repentance of her brother, the wrath of Devakī was pacified, and she gave him leave (to go). Vasudeva smiled and spoke to him:

devanagari: एवमेतन्महाभाग यथा वदसि देहिनाम् । अज्ञानप्रभवाहंधीः स्वपरेति भिदा यतः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: evametanmahābhāga yathā vadasi dehinām ajñānaprabhavāhaṃdhīḥ svapareti bhidā yataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: “Oh highly fortunate Kaṃsa! It is exactly as you say. The notion of creatures that they are identical with the body is born of ignorance, and that is the cause of the difference in regarding this as one’s own and that as another’s.

devanagari: शोकहर्षभयद्वेष लोभमोहमदान्विताः । मिथो घ्नन्तं न पश्यन्ति भावैर्भावं पृथग्दृशः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: śokaharṣabhayadveṣa lobhamohamadānvitāḥ mitho ghnantaṃ na paśyanti bhāvairbhāvaṃ pṛthagdṛśaḥ
translation: Persons possessing differentiating outlook and sense of distinction and suffering from grief, joy, fear, malice, covetousness, delusion and pride do not perceive the self-existent Reality (God) who brings about the destruction of beings through their mutual action (against each other), caused by their passions.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । कंस एवं प्रसन्नाभ्यां विशुद्धं प्रतिभाषितः । देवकी वसुदेवाभ्यां अनुज्ञातो आविशत् गृहम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca kaṃsa evaṃ prasannābhyāṃ viśuddhaṃ pratibhāṣitaḥ devakī vasudevābhyāṃ anujñāto āviśat gṛham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Thus sincerely addressed by kindly disposed Devakī and Vasudeva, Kaṃsa took leave of them and returned to his palace.

devanagari: तस्यां रात्र्यां व्यतीतायां कंस आहूय मंत्रिणः । तेभ्य आचष्ट तत् सर्वं यदुक्तं योगनिद्रया ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyāṃ rātryāṃ vyatītāyāṃ kaṃsa āhūya maṃtriṇaḥ tebhya ācaṣṭa tat sarvaṃ yaduktaṃ yoganidrayā

translation: When the night was over, Kaṃsa summoned his ministers and reported to them everything that had been said by Yoganidrā (the trance—sleep of Lord Viṣṇu).

devanagari: आकर्ण्य भर्तुर्गदितं तं ऊचुः देवशत्रवः । देवान् प्रति कृतामर्षा दैतेया नातिकोविदाः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: ākarṇya bharturgaditaṃ taṃ ūcuḥ devaśatravaḥ devān prati kṛtāmarṣā daiteyā nātikovidāḥ

[imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]

translation: Hearing the narration of their master, the demons (sons of Diti), the (sworn) enemies of gods, who cherished deep anger against the celestials, but were not foresighted spoke to him.

devanagari: एवं चेत्तर्हि भोजेन्द्र पुरग्राम व्रजादिषु । अनिर्दशान् निर्दशांश्च हनिष्यामोऽद्य वै शिशून् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ cettarhi bhojendra puragrāma vrajādiṣu anirdaśān nirdaśāṃśca haniṣyāmo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: “If this be the case, Oh Lord of Bhojas, we shall verily massacre today all children of ten days or less (dwelling) in cities, towns, cowherds’ camps and (such) other places.

devanagari: किं उद्यमैः करिष्यन्ति देवाः समरभीरवः । नित्यं उद्विग्नमनसो ज्याघोषैर्धनुषस्तव ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ udyamaiḥ kariṣyanti devāḥ samarabhīravaḥ nityaṃ udvignamanaso jyāghoṣairdhanuṣastava

[vocative single from √ kṝ class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ kṝ class 9 verb]

translation: What can the gods do with all their endeavours. They are cowards on the battlefield. They live in perpetual terror of the twanging of your bow-string.

devanagari: अस्यतस्ते शरव्रातैः हन्यमानाः समन्ततः । जिजीविषव उत्सृज्य पलायनपरा ययुः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: asyataste śaravrātaiḥ hanyamānāḥ samantataḥ jijīviṣava utsṛjya palāyanaparā yayuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 2 verb]

[nominative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: Being beaten on all sides with volleys of arrows discharged by you, they, being anxious to save their lives, deserted the battlefield and took to their heels.

devanagari: केचित् प्राञ्जलयो दीना न्यस्तशस्त्रा दिवौकसः । मुक्तकच्छशिखाः केचिद् भीताः स्म इति वादिनः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: kecit prāñjalayo dīnā nyastaśastrā divaukasaḥ muktakacchaśikhāḥ kecid bhītāḥ sma iti vādinaḥ
translation: Some gods in their wretched plight laid down their arms and stood with folded palms, while others with their loincloth untucked and hair on the head untied and dishevelled announced, “We are afraid.”

devanagari: न त्वं विस्मृतशस्त्रास्त्रान् विरथान् भयसंवृतान् । हंस्यन्यासक्तविमुखान् भग्नचापानयुध्यतः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: na tvaṃ vismṛtaśastrāstrān virathān bhayasaṃvṛtān haṃsyanyāsaktavimukhān bhagnacāpānayudhyataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: You do not strike at those (enemies) who have forgotten their arms and missiles or have lost their chariots or are terror-stricken or are attending to something else or have turned away (from the battlefield) or have their bows broken or have given up fighting.

devanagari: किं क्षेमशूरैर्विबुधैः असंयुगविकत्थनैः । रहोजुषा किं हरिणा शंभुना वा वनौकसा । किं इंद्रेणाल्पवीर्येण ब्रह्मणा वा तपस्यता ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ kṣemaśūrairvibudhaiḥ asaṃyugavikatthanaiḥ rahojuṣā kiṃ hariṇā śaṃbhunā vā vanaukasā kiṃ iṃdreṇālpavīryeṇa brahmaṇā vā tapasyatā
translation: What can gods, the warriors in peace times aṇd boastful outside the battlefield, do (to harm us)? Of what account is Hari who lies in concealment or Śiva who dwells in a forest? Why care for Indra of poor strength or Brahmā (always) absorbed in penance?

devanagari: तथापि देवाः सापत्‍न्यान् नोपेक्ष्या इति मन्महे । ततः तन्मूलखनने नियुंक्ष्वास्मान् अनुव्रतान् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: tathāpi devāḥ sāpat‍nyān nopekṣyā iti manmahe tataḥ tanmūlakhanane niyuṃkṣvāsmān anuvratān
translation: We, however, think that gods due to their (inborn) enmity, should not be ignored. Therefore, deploy us, your obedient servants to dig out and destroy even their roots.

devanagari: यथामयोंऽगे समुपेक्षितो नृभिः । न शक्यते रूढपदश्चिकित्सितुम् । यथेन्द्रियग्राम उपेक्षितस्तथा । रिपुर्महान् बद्धबलो न चाल्यते ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: yathāmayoṃ na śakyate rūḍhapadaścikitsitum yathendriyagrāma upekṣitastathā ripurmahān baddhabalo na cālyate
translation: Just as a disease in the body, if neglected, strikes deep roots and becomes impossible to be cured or the senses if uncontrolled and ignored (become unmanageable), so the enemy, if ignored, grows in strength and cannot be dislodged.

devanagari: मूलं हि विष्णुः देवानां यत्र धर्मः सनातनः । तस्य च ब्रह्मगोविप्राः तपो यज्ञाः सदक्षिणाः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: mūlaṃ hi viṣṇuḥ devānāṃ yatra dharmaḥ sanātanaḥ tasya ca brahmagoviprāḥ tapo yajñāḥ sadakṣiṇāḥ
translation: Viṣṇu is the root (the main stay) of all gods. Where there is Viṣṇu, there abides the eternal righteousness (dharma). The roots of the dharma are the Vedas, cows, Brāhmaṇas, asceticism and sacrifices with donation of liberal gifts.

devanagari: तस्मात्सर्वात्मना राजन् ब्राह्मणान् ब्रह्मवादिनः । तपस्विनो यज्ञशीलान् गाश्च हन्मो हविर्दुघाः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: tasmātsarvātmanā rājan brāhmaṇān brahmavādinaḥ tapasvino yajñaśīlān gāśca hanmo havirdughāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Therefore, Oh King, we shall kill by all (possible) means Brāhmaṇas, the reciters of the Vedas, the ascetics performing penance and those continually engaged in sacrificial acts, and cows yielding all the materials for sacrificial oblations.

devanagari: विप्रा गावश्च वेदाश्च तपः सत्यं दमः शमः । श्रद्धा दया तितिक्षा च क्रतवश्च हरेस्तनूः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: viprā gāvaśca vedāśca tapaḥ satyaṃ damaḥ śamaḥ śraddhā dayā titikṣā ca kratavaśca harestanūḥ
translation: Brāhmaṇas, cows, the Vedas, penance, truthfulness, control of senses, serenity of mind, faith, mercy, endurance and the sacrifices constitute the body of Hari.

devanagari: स हि सर्वसुराध्यक्षो ह्यसुरद्विड् गुहाशयः । तन्मूला देवताः सर्वाः सेश्वराः सचतुर्मुखाः । अयं वै तद्वधोपायो यद्ऋषीणां विहिंसनम् ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: sa hi sarvasurādhyakṣo hyasuradviḍ guhāśayaḥ tanmūlā devatāḥ sarvāḥ seśvarāḥ sacaturmukhāḥ ayaṃ vai tadvadhopāyo yadṛṣīṇāṃ vihiṃsanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: For he is the Supreme Ruler of all gods, the enemy of Asuras, the indweller in the hearts of all. All divinities including Lord Śiva and the four-faced god Brahmā depend on him as their support (root). Hence extirpation (through persecution) of sages is really the only means of killing him.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एवं दुर्मंत्रिभिः कंसः सह सम्मन्त्र्य दुर्मतिः । ब्रह्महिंसां हितं मेने कालपाशावृतोऽसुरः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca evaṃ durmaṃtribhiḥ kaṃsaḥ saha sammantrya durmatiḥ brahmahiṃsāṃ hitaṃ mene kālapāśāvṛto

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ hi class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ hi class 5 verb]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having thus conferred with his wicked ministers, the evil-minded Kaṃsa who was enmeshed in the snare of Death, considered massacre of Brāhmaṇas as conducive to his good.

devanagari: सन्दिश्य साधुलोकस्य कदने कदनप्रियान् । कामरूपधरान् दिक्षु दानवान् गृहमाविशत् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: sandiśya sādhulokasya kadane kadanapriyān kāmarūpadharān dikṣu dānavān gṛhamāviśat
translation: Having commissioned Dānavas who were naturally disposed to persecute others and who could assume any form at will, for harassing the good people in all directions, Kaṃsa entered his palace.

devanagari: ते वै रजःप्रकृतयः तमसा मूढचेतसः । सतां विद्वेषमाचेरुः आरात् आगतमृत्यवः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: te vai rajaḥprakṛtayaḥ tamasā mūḍhacetasaḥ satāṃ vidveṣamāceruḥ ārāt āgatamṛtyavaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[imperfect middle first single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Constituted as they were by predominantly rājasic nature, and with their minds deluded by tamas, they started their campaign of hatred of the good as their death was imminent.

devanagari: आयुः श्रियं यशो धर्मं लोकानाशिष एव च । हन्ति श्रेयांसि सर्वाणि पुंसो महदतिक्रमः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: āyuḥ śriyaṃ yaśo dharmaṃ lokānāśiṣa eva ca hanti śreyāṃsi sarvāṇi puṃso mahadatikramaḥ
translation: Serious violations of the great destroy one’s span of life, prosperity, glory, righteousness, (attainment of) heavenly regions, and all other blessings.
